source_text: If the client opts to do so, it MUST supply both the "pre_shared_key" and "early_data" extensions.
rule_statement: If the client sends early data (0-RTT), it MUST include both the pre_shared_key and early_data extensions in ClientHello.
expected_value: both pre_shared_key and early_data extensions present
checkability_rationale: Direct presence check of both extensions when 0-RTT is used.

source_text: The PSK used to encrypt the early data MUST be the first PSK listed in the client's "pre_shared_key" extension.
rule_statement: The PSK used for early data encryption MUST be the first identity in the client's pre_shared_key extension.
expected_value: first identity is the one used for 0-RTT
checkability_rationale: Can verify the first PSK identity is used for 0-RTT key derivation.

source_text: For PSKs provisioned via NewSessionTicket, a server MUST validate that the ticket age for the selected PSK identity (computed by subtracting ticket_age_add from PskIdentity.obfuscated_ticket_age modulo 2^32) is within a small tolerance of the time since the ticket was issued (see Section 8).
rule_statement: For ticket-based PSKs, the server MUST validate that the ticket age is within a small tolerance of the time since the ticket was issued.
applies_when: Server processes a PSK identity from NewSessionTicket for 0-RTT
expected_value: within tolerance of actual ticket age
checkability_rationale: Can verify arithmetic and tolerance check in server's 0-RTT acceptance code.
ambiguity_notes: The 'small tolerance' is not precisely defined in this section.

source_text: A client MUST NOT include the "early_data" extension in its followup ClientHello.
rule_statement: A client MUST NOT include the early_data extension in its second ClientHello (after HelloRetryRequest).
applies_when: Client constructs second ClientHello after HelloRetryRequest
checkability_rationale: Can verify extension is removed from second ClientHello.

source_text: In order to accept early data, the server MUST have accepted a PSK cipher suite and selected the first key offered in the client's "pre_shared_key" extension. In addition, it MUST verify that the following values are the same as those associated with the selected PSK: The TLS version number, The selected cipher suite, The selected ALPN protocol, if any
rule_statement: To accept early data, the server MUST have selected the first PSK and MUST verify that TLS version, cipher suite, and ALPN protocol match those associated with the PSK.
expected_value: version, cipher suite, and ALPN match PSK-associated values
checkability_rationale: Can verify server checks version/cipher/ALPN consistency before accepting early data.

source_text: If any of these checks fail, the server MUST NOT respond with the extension and must discard all the first-flight data using one of the first two mechanisms listed above (thus falling back to 1-RTT or 2-RTT).
rule_statement: If version/cipher/ALPN checks fail for early data, the server MUST NOT respond with the early_data extension and must fall back to 1-RTT or 2-RTT.
checkability_rationale: Can verify server omits early_data extension and discards first-flight data on validation failure.

source_text: If the server chooses to accept the "early_data" extension, then it MUST comply with the same error-handling requirements specified for all records when processing early data records. Specifically, if the server fails to decrypt a 0-RTT record following an accepted "early_data" extension, it MUST terminate the connection with a "bad_record_mac" alert as per Section 5.2.
rule_statement: If the server accepted early data but fails to decrypt a 0-RTT record, it MUST terminate the connection with a bad_record_mac alert.
applies_when: Server accepted early_data but fails to decrypt a 0-RTT record
checkability_rationale: Can verify server sends bad_record_mac on 0-RTT decrypt failure after acceptance.

source_text: A server which receives an "early_data" extension MUST behave in one of three ways:
rule_statement: A server receiving early_data MUST either: ignore and do 1-RTT, send HelloRetryRequest, or accept by including early_data in EncryptedExtensions.
applies_when: Server receives ClientHello with early_data extension
checkability_rationale: Can verify server follows one of the three paths, but proving no other path exists is broader.
